transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hi.
Sorry, I joined like four wrong Zooms before I found the right link, but I'm here.
How's everyone doing?
Good.
How are you?
Good.
Good.
I just ran through that blog.
That's very exciting.
What do you think?
I thought it was great.
I thought it was awesome.
I am curious, like, I saw all the stuff at the top, which is awesome, like meta description, URL, excellent.
I added the keyword in just so that I remember.
And then, like, if we have a sense of audience when we're doing these, it would be great to know just who we're targeting so that we keep them in mind.
But overall, I left some suggested edits in there, just ways to kind of make it sound more human.
No one will be shocked if we use AI to write content as an AI company, but I always like to add a little more touching touch in there.
But let me know what you think of the suggestions.
We can work in the doc if that works for you, or we can run through it, whatever you have planned for today.
Okay.
Cool.
I think we can go through your comments really quickly.
Let me just share my screen.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, can definitely add the, like, the audience here.
That's actually something that we typically do, but it slipped my mind when I was delivering this.
Okay, what is Rack?
Sorry, I have to remove our faces so that I can see your comment.
Okay.
Okay, so you don't, you're not sure if you can use this?
Yeah, I know we can use their logo, but I just have to confirm that we can actually speak to what they use us for specifically.
We can, but it's currently on the website too.
Sweet.
Yeah.
So ignore me, we can use this.
Okay, okay, cool.
Yeah, it was, I was looking at the customer success stories on the site and all of them were for, like, the enterprise AI solution.
And this was the one that was mentioned on the API search page.
Okay, so yeah.
I'll get in.
If we need another, I think we could also use win.
InSurf here, they're like another API customer that we could leverage.
Cool.
Yeah, okay, that makes sense.
Like this is, yeah, we can make this like more conversational.
Yeah, I do agree.
Like we try to like strike a balance between like writing an active voice and kind of chunking information and then like actually like using transitions.
So I do like gravitate more towards like the more human writing, but I did want to like provide you with like a different option.
So I think we could just like, yeah, make this like more conversational and just connect the dots for the readers.
I think that would be make this section better.
Totally, same for like that, the intro, I usually do like at least a couple sentences just to make it look a little more structured.
But yeah.
Yeah, yeah, that makes sense.

Okay, this is, like, it's important that we get, like, also, like, it's not just the calibration blog, it's not just for, like, the voice and the writing style, but it's also, like, to get the information right.
So we do have a fact checker in our workflow.
But if, you know, there's anything that's inaccurate, like, just flag it to us.
We are working on improving the article generation pipeline.

So we are, like, transitioning to an agentic pipeline, which has a, like, a more advanced fact checker that can actually, like, it generates a research document, and then it goes back in and, like, does additional rounds of, like, verification on the data so that it, and it ranks the data it collected.
So it's, like, grades it on, like, a scale of one to five based on the accuracy and the relevance.
So, if there's anything here that you think is, like, maybe outdated or is just, like, missing context, you can flag it for us.
Index.
Feels like using the word in the definition.
Oh, yeah, yeah.
Yeah, so it's, like, a different word for it or a clarify.
Synonym, yeah.
I like the metaphor, similes.
It helps me at least understand this stuff better, so I'm assuming it would help.
Okay.
Okay, yeah, that's great.

I think we had, like, an earlier version of the writing guidelines that we were using this summer had, like, a whole section on metaphors and similes, and then, yeah, it kind of went overboard, and it was just using them in every other paragraph, so we had to cut them down significantly.
So, they do appear from time to time, but we really had to kind of tone it down, because it was too much.
And then, okay, lightweight example.
Okay, we can provide an example here.
Okay.
Oh, you don't want to mention competitors?
Okay.
Yeah, I would stay, unless it's, like, a direct eval of, like, how we're better, I would just stay away from Toss, and then that section about you.com is also in the conclusion, so.
Okay, okay, that works.
I think maybe we can see what else, like, maybe we can actually expand the you.com section.

So, like, we have a conclusion that's, like, start building, it's, like, brief, we can, like, shorten it further to avoid repetition, but then we can turn this section into one that talks more about you.com's API and how it works.
I think that would be, like, more valuable, and it, you know, provides, like, more information about the product, so.
Yeah, something we could do, too, that just happened is our hackathon ended.
Okay.
And our, the winner of the hackathon used our search API, so that could be something that just gets plugged.
In here.
There's a blog post that was just published yesterday on the winning hack.
So that'd be a nice tie in.
But from like a length perspective, I think even if we cut that whole section out and didn't add anything in, I think it's still long enough.
Like it's around 1000 words still, which is pretty good.
So I wouldn't like if we're just trying to fill to get length that I wouldn't worry about it too much.
Okay.
Okay.
I did see when it comes to just like general topic selection for the blog, there were some articles that we suggested that do mention your competitors explicitly.
What are your thoughts on that?
If it's a direct like versus of like how we stack up, that's totally fine.
We love to do those.
If it's like a list of tools and we're one of many and the competitors are also on the list, I would stay away from that just because we want to be like the best.
And the only choice and the top choice and all of that.
And yeah, so that's sort of how I would think about it.
But if there is a certain case where you think it would make more sense to list out competitors, let me know.
Obviously, we can examine on a case by case.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I think what we can do in those types of like best of listicles, we can, we like to like, we can categorize them.
So we could say like, best overall is you.com.
And then we can mention other competitors and be like, okay, they work for this and this use case.
But we don't have to, mean, typically, when we do publish them, the competitor sections are more, they're just, you know, shorter, you kind of, cover like the basic features and the functionalities.
But we try to kind of not really talk about their advantages or really disadvantages because that can be a little bit tricky.
Like depending on, um.
You know, the company and if they really like, I've written content for companies that never wanted to mention any of their competitors cons because they were afraid of their legal departments.
So that's just something to keep in mind.
But I think for the best of listicles, I think that is kind of the structure that we take.

And I do like to incorporate those types of articles in the content strategy, just because they do get traffic, they do get cited by LLMs, and your competitors are probably going to be publishing them as well.
So you might as well be on the SERP.
And as they like come through, we can always look at them, you know, in draft form and see what we think.
And so keep in mind, too, is we will have, at some point, I know, Seher, we're working on the evals page, but we'll have a page that has updated numbers on how we compare to our competitors.
And we can use that as a basis for a lot of these listicles.
Yeah.
No, that's great.
I love those.
I mean, you know, the more resources you have on your site that we can point people to, the better.
I know you already have like these versus articles on your site, which is great.
But, you know, the more they show up, the better.
We did an article for Lovable, published a lot of articles for Lovable recently for where we were just comparing them against their top competitors.
And they're already being cited by ChatGPT.
Like we just found like when we were doing research for a different article, it was cited and it was published like last week or two weeks ago.
So the time to like visibility is very short.

So yeah, that's kind of something that I did want to like, I think for a lot of these like verses, and I'm glad that you are publishing that type of content, because I do run into companies that just don't want to do it for whatever reason.

AI visibility.

think we're in the ask for forgiveness mindset.

three weeks?

August?
the interesting part is, is it looks like it went down from July to August, but the blog actually remained in HubSpot up until October.

Okay.
I think it's not going to be, since the redirect is there, any, like, it's, these, like, articles are going to be ranked.

bottom, yeah, and, like, the the bottom dropped out of that funnel, which, yeah, yeah, I think I can look into this, but I think Talal can also do, like, a more technical SEO audit, just because of the migration, and there are always things that come up in a migration that affect technical SEO.
So, yeah, cool, that would be awesome.
Great, all right, and then, did I show you your Scrunch dashboard?
I don't think I did.
Not today, no.

No, okay, yeah, so we do have this set up for you, and this is something that is accessible just to GrowthX email, but I can share PDF versions of the report with you on a weekly basis, so you'll have access to the data.
So, Scrunch is very similar to tools like Profound, and it basically, how it works.

We give it a description of your company, we give it a description of your target audience, and then we provide a list of the top competitors, and then it will automatically generate 35 base prompts to track.

And then each of those base prompts gets adapted to different LLMs, like Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, and then it sends those prompts to those LLMs, collects the responses, analyzes them, and then breaks down your visibility.

However, we can upload many more prompts here, I've seen, like, accounts that are tracking thousands of prompts, so we can use AI to generate additional prompts that we think your audience would be using when, you know, in different LLMs, or you can provide us, like, a list of questions that come up pretty frequently from your customers.
I mean, we did talk about this, Brooke, I think, last time you did say that...

So, it's pretty normal.
we're if

are these.
these.

was more important to get the calibration reviewed.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content calibration and address SEO performance issues.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Calibration Finalized:** The "calibration blog" review set the voice (human, conversational) and competitor strategy (direct comparisons only), unblocking content production.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=57.0)
  - [**SEO Performance Issue Identified:** A post-migration (Oct 14) SEMrush data discrepancy (only 2 ranking blog pages vs. 25 in GSC) was traced to a 2–3 week redirect gap; GrowthX will perform a technical SEO audit to diagnose.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=975.0)
  - [**AI Visibility Tracking Launched:** The Scrunch dashboard is live, showing You.com's 2% citation rate (vs. 3% for competitors) and providing data to inform future topic selection.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1351.0)
  - [**Production Timeline Set:** After final calibration, GrowthX will deliver drafts for 3 articles next week, followed by 5 the week after.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=725.0)

## Topics

### SEO Performance & Technical Audit

  - [**Problem:** A SEMrush data discrepancy shows only 2 ranking blog pages, while Google Search Console (GSC) shows 25.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=975.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** The issue is linked to the blog's migration from HubSpot to Webflow on Oct 14, which created a 2–3 week redirect gap.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1175.0)
  - [**Impact:** The redirect gap caused a significant drop in keyword rankings (especially top-10 positions) that coincided with a July Google Core update.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1278.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX will conduct a technical SEO audit to diagnose the issue and create a plan to recover lost rankings.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)

### Content Calibration & Strategy

  - [**Voice & Tone:** The "calibration blog" review established a human, conversational voice.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=57.0)
  - [**Competitor Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
      - [**Direct Comparisons:** Permitted for "vs." articles (e.g., You.com vs. X).](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)
      - [**"Best of" Lists:** Position You.com as the "best overall" and competitors as niche alternatives.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This strategy captures traffic, secures LLM citations, and proactively counters competitor messaging.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=557.0)
  - [**Content Enhancements:**](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
      - [**Customer Examples:** Add Rack and InSurf as API customer examples.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
      - [**Hackathon Tie-in:** Reference the winning hackathon project, which used the Search API.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=410.0)
      - [**Internal Linking:** Add internal links to older content to help regain lost SEO authority.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1294.0)

### AI Visibility Tracking (Scrunch)

  - [**Purpose:** The Scrunch dashboard measures You.com's visibility in LLM responses against competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1351.0)
  - [**Current Metrics:**](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1461.0)
      - [**Citation Rate:** 2% (vs. 3% for competitors).](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1461.0)
      - **Position:** Ranks \#1 in responses 50% of the time.
  - [**Data Usage:** The data will inform topic selection, identifying high-performing areas to support and low-visibility areas to target.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)
  - [**Looker Dashboard:** A 7-page Looker dashboard is in development to track content performance, including a page dedicated to LLM referral traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)

## Next Steps

      - [Deliver drafts for 3 articles next week, followed by 5 the week after.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=725.0)
      - [Provide weekly Scrunch reports (PDF) and the list of tracked prompts to Brooke and Seher.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=1351.0)
      - [Share AI-generated featured image examples from Katya.](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
  - [**You.com:**](https://fathom.video/share/QjseyRrExTXy3xhgkbuhS1SY6a_oJ7Px?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
